topic.what: Multi-party private set union (PSU) for privacy-preserving entity alignment in vertical federated learning without revealing intersection membership
topic.how: Commutative encryption in quadratic residues group with n-gram tokenization enabling both exact and noisy identifier matching across P parties
topic.why_matters: Prevents sensitive membership inference attacks inherent in PSI-based entity alignment while enabling cross-organizational VFL in healthcare, finance, and fraud detection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of interaction phases between parties
characteristics.evidence: Algorithm 1 and 3 show first round (commutative encryption), union computation (second round), and matching phase
characteristics.context: Fixed overhead independent of dataset size

characteristics.evidence: Section 4 discusses semi-honest security under DDH assumption in QR(Z*p)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Token overlap ratio required for matching
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.4 defines threshold lambda controlling false positive/negative trade-off
characteristics.context: Must be tuned per deployment; adaptive selection mentioned as future work

characteristics.what_it_measures: N-gram length used for robust matching
characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior noisy matching in PSU
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.1 describes n-gram tokenization with sliding window approach
characteristics.context: 3-grams referenced as good compromise; must agree on n across parties

characteristics.evidence: Section 4 defines safe prime p and quadratic residue group; security relies on DDH

characteristics.what_it_measures: Error type prioritized in noisy matching
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.1 discusses E1 (false negatives) vs E2 (false positives) tradeoff

novelty: Multi-party generalization of two-party PSU without trusted coordinator
N-gram tokenization enabling typo-tolerant private matching
Order-preserving and unordered PSU variants in single framework
Commutative encryption protocol for arbitrary P parties with DDH security
open_problems: Computational cost of comparison step for noisy matching at scale
Adaptive threshold selection without validation data
False positive reduction in Bloom filter representation
Integration complexity with existing VFL training pipelines
